entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop Oryza sativa used in the study.

entities.name: MICRORNAS
entities.description: Small noncoding regulatory RNAs that control gene expression by mRNA degradation or translational repression.

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION
entities.description: The process by which information from a gene is used in the synthesis of a functional gene product.

entities.name: MRNA DEGRADATION
entities.description: A mechanism by which microRNAs control gene expression.

entities.name: TRANSLATIONAL REPRESSION
entities.description: A mechanism by which microRNAs control gene expression.

entities.name: DEVELOPMENTAL TIMING
entities.description: A biological process regulated by microRNAs.

entities.name: STRESS RESPONSE
entities.description: A biological process regulated by microRNAs.

entities.name: RIBOSOMAL DNA
entities.description: The DNA sequence that codes for ribosomal RNA, found to be the origin of certain miRNAs.

entities.name: DEEP SEQUENCING
entities.description: A high-throughput sequencing method used to identify small RNA datasets.

entities.name: PUTATIVE MIRNAS
entities.description: Twelve identified miRNAs, ten of which originated from rDNA of rice chromosome 9.

entities.name: RICE CHROMOSOME 9
entities.description: The specific chromosomal location in rice where the rDNA-derived miRNAs originate.

entities.name: HEAT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of rice cultivars to withstand high temperature stress.

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURE STRESS
entities.description: The environmental condition of high temperature affecting rice seedlings.

entities.name: EXPRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of analyzing the expression levels of miRNAs and their target genes.
